addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

609	void editorUpdateRow(erow *row)
610	    /* The row under analysis is a well-formed, self-contained object. */
611	    __CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(row, sizeof(*row)))
612	    /* A single-character row.  Sizes must be concrete constants: an is_fresh
613	     * buffer with a symbolic size, and a malloc with a symbolic size, both
614	     * yield objects whose extent CBMC does not bound, which silently disables
615	     * the out-of-bounds checks that give this proof its mutation-killing
616	     * strength.  One character is enough to drive every branch of the
617	     * render loop (tab vs non-tab) while keeping render (malloc'd to exactly
618	     * fit) tightly bounded so every off-by-one access is caught, and it makes
619	     * the exact rendered size expressible in `ensures` (see below). */
620	    __CPROVER_requires(row->size == 1)
621	    /* chars is a tight, bounds-tracked buffer of exactly `size` bytes, so any
622	     * read past the end (e.g. a mutated `j <= size` loop bound) is caught. */
623	    __CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(row->chars, 1))
624	    /* render starts unset, matching the freshly-inserted-row case; free(NULL)
625	     * is a no-op so no `frees` clause is needed. */
626	    __CPROVER_requires(row->render == NULL)
627	    /* hl is a tight, bounds-tracked buffer.  The tail call to
628	     * editorUpdateSyntax does realloc(hl, rsize) (the realloc model returns it
629	     * unchanged) then memset(hl, .., rsize); rsize is at most 7 for a
630	     * single-char row, so 8 bytes covers it and keeps every hl access checked. */
631	    __CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(row->hl, 8))
632	    /* No syntax definition: editorUpdateSyntax then reduces to realloc+memset
633	     * of hl and returns immediately, so this proof stays bounded and focuses on
634	     * editorUpdateRow's own render logic. */
635	    __CPROVER_requires(E.syntax == NULL)
636	    __CPROVER_assigns(row->render, row->rsize, row->hl,
637	                      __CPROVER_object_whole(row->hl))
638	    /* render is reallocated to a valid, NUL-terminated buffer. */
639	    __CPROVER_ensures(row->render != NULL)
640	    __CPROVER_ensures(row->render[row->rsize] == '\0')
641	    /* Exact rendered size: a leading TAB expands to the next 8-column stop
642	     * (7 chars for the buggy col-0 case in this code), every other character
643	     * renders to itself (1 char).  Pinning rsize exactly kills mutants that
644	     * change the number of spaces emitted per tab or the render loop's trip
645	     * count without overflowing the buffer. */
646	    __CPROVER_ensures(row->chars[0] == TAB ? row->rsize == 7 : row->rsize == 1)
647	{
648	    unsigned int tabs = 0, nonprint = 0;
649	    int j, idx;
650	
651	   /* Create a version of the row we can directly print on the screen,
652	     * respecting tabs, substituting non printable characters with '?'. */
653	    free(row->render);
654	    for (j = 0; j < row->size; j++)
655	        if (row->chars[j] == TAB) tabs++;
656	
657	    unsigned long long allocsize =
658	        (unsigned long long) row->size + tabs*8 + nonprint*9 + 1;
659	    if (allocsize > UINT32_MAX) {
660	        printf("Some line of the edited file is too long for kilo\n");
661	        exit(1);
662	    }
663	
664	    row->render = malloc(row->size + tabs*8 + nonprint*9 + 1);
665	    idx = 0;
666	    for (j = 0; j < row->size; j++) {
667	        if (row->chars[j] == TAB) {
668	            row->render[idx++] = ' ';
669	            while((idx+1) % 8 != 0) row->render[idx++] = ' ';
670	        } else {
671	            row->render[idx++] = row->chars[j];
672	        }
673	    }
674	    row->rsize = idx;
675	    row->render[idx] = '\0';
676	
677	    /* Update the syntax highlighting attributes of the row. */
678	    editorUpdateSyntax(row);
679	}

681	/* Insert a row at the specified position, shifting the other rows on the bottom
682	 * if required. */
683	void editorInsertRow(int at, char *s, size_t len)
684	    /* The editor holds a well-formed array of exactly E.numrows rows.  Pinning
685	     * the count to a small concrete constant (rather than a symbolic size) keeps
686	     * E.row a tightly bounds-tracked object so every shift/realloc/index access
687	     * is checked, and lets the post-state row indices be pinned exactly in the
688	     * `ensures` below -- the same modeling choice editorUpdateRow makes with
689	     * row->size == 1.  Two pre-existing rows is the smallest count that still
690	     * exercises a genuine middle-insert (at == 1) and distinguishes the shift
691	     * loop's trip count from its mutants. */
692	    __CPROVER_requires(E.numrows == 2)
693	    __CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(E.row, sizeof(erow) * (size_t)E.numrows))
694	    /* The maintained data-structure invariant: each row's stored idx equals its
695	     * position.  Stating it concretely both keeps the idx++ arithmetic far from
696	     * overflow and lets the post-state indices be checked exactly. */
697	    __CPROVER_requires(E.row[0].idx == 0)
698	    __CPROVER_requires(E.row[1].idx == 1)
699	    /* dirty is bounded away from overflow so the body's E.dirty++ is well-defined. */
700	    __CPROVER_requires(0 <= E.dirty && E.dirty < 1000000)
701	    /* Insert position is in range, so the function never early-returns and the
702	     * full insert (shift + new row + count bump) is always exercised.  Leaving
703	     * `at` symbolic over {0,1,2} covers front-insert, middle-insert and append. */
704	    __CPROVER_requires(0 <= at && at <= E.numrows)
705	    /* Source string is a tight, bounds-tracked buffer of exactly len+1 bytes
706	     * (content + the caller-provided terminator), matching memcpy(...,len+1).
707	     * A symbolic-but-bounded length keeps the malloc/memcpy bounds checked. */
708	    __CPROVER_requires(1 <= len && len <= 2)
709	    __CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, len + 1))
710	    /* realloc reassigns the E.row pointer and frees the old array; E.numrows and
711	     * E.dirty are the only other pre-existing locations written.  The new array
712	     * object and the new row's chars buffer are allocated inside the function and
713	     * are therefore implicitly assignable. */
714	    __CPROVER_assigns(E.row, E.numrows, E.dirty, __CPROVER_object_whole(E.row))
715	    __CPROVER_frees(E.row)
716	    /* The row count and dirty flag each advance by exactly one: kills every
717	     * mutant that turns the range guard into one that early-returns. */
718	    __CPROVER_ensures(E.numrows == __CPROVER_old(E.numrows) + 1)
719	    __CPROVER_ensures(E.dirty == __CPROVER_old(E.dirty) + 1)
720	    /* The freshly inserted row lands at slot `at` with the requested size, index
721	     * and an exact byte-for-byte copy of s over all len+1 bytes.  The content
722	     * clause kills the malloc(len-1)/memcpy(len-1) mutants. */
723	    __CPROVER_ensures(E.row[at].size == (int)len)
724	    __CPROVER_ensures(E.row[at].idx == at)
725	    __CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_forall { size_t k; (k <= len) ==> E.row[at].chars[k] == s[k] })
726	    /* Rows below the insertion point keep their index; rows at/above it are
727	     * shifted up one slot and re-indexed.  With two starting rows the final
728	     * indices are always 0,1,2 in order -- pinning each slot kills the shift
729	     * loop's bound/offset mutants and the memmove offset/size mutants. */
730	    __CPROVER_ensures((at > 0) ==> E.row[0].idx == 0)
731	    __CPROVER_ensures((at > 1) ==> E.row[1].idx == 1)
732	    __CPROVER_ensures((at < 1) ==> E.row[1].idx == 1)
733	    __CPROVER_ensures((at < 2) ==> E.row[2].idx == 2)
734	{
735	    if (at > E.numrows) return;
736	    E.row = realloc(E.row,sizeof(erow)*(E.numrows+1));
737	    if (at != E.numrows) {
738	        memmove(E.row+at+1,E.row+at,sizeof(E.row[0])*(E.numrows-at));

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# Verify editorDelRow with CBMC.
3	# Usage: ./verify_editorDelRow.sh [unwind]
4	set -e
5	# The CBMC built-in memmove copies byte-by-byte in an internal loop, so --unwind
6	# must exceed the largest copy this harness performs: deleting row 0 of three
7	# shifts two erow structs = 2*sizeof(erow) = 96 bytes.  128 covers that with
8	# margin while the editor's own idx loop runs at most a couple of iterations.
9	UNWIND=${1:-128}
10	FUNCTION=editorDelRow
11	FILE=kilo.c
12	rm -f ${FUNCTION}.goto checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto
13	# editorDelRow calls editorFreeRow (replaced by its contract) and memmove
14	# (CBMC built-in, which copies and bounds-checks correctly). No realloc here,
15	# so we do NOT link any string.c stub.
16	goto-cc -o ${FUNCTION}.goto "$FILE" --function ${FUNCTION}
17	goto-instrument --unwind ${UNWIND} --unwinding-assertions ${FUNCTION}.goto ${FUNCTION}.goto
18	# Inline editorFreeRow's body (three free() calls) rather than replacing it with
19	# its contract: the contract's `ensures __CPROVER_was_freed(...)` has no body
20	# under --replace-call-with-contract.  The precondition makes each row buffer a
21	# fresh, offset-zero heap object, so the inlined free()s are well-defined and
22	# editorDelRow's frees clause is checked against the real deallocations.
23	goto-instrument --nondet-static \
24	  --enforce-contract ${FUNCTION} ${FUNCTION}.goto checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto
25	cbmc checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto --function ${FUNCTION} \
26	  --no-malloc-may-fail --bounds-check --pointer-check --pointer-primitive-check \
27	  --pointer-overflow-check --conversion-check --signed-overflow-check --unsigned-overflow-check
28

#!/bin/bash
# Verify editorDelRow with CBMC.
# Usage: ./verify_editorDelRow.sh [unwind]
set -e
# The CBMC built-in memmove copies byte-by-byte in an internal loop, so --unwind
# must exceed the largest copy this harness performs: deleting row 0 of three
# shifts two erow structs = 2*sizeof(erow) = 96 bytes.  128 covers that with
# margin while the editor's own idx loop runs at most a couple of iterations.
UNWIND=${1:-128}
FUNCTION=editorDelRow
FILE=kilo.c
rm -f ${FUNCTION}.goto checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto
# editorDelRow calls editorFreeRow (replaced by its contract) and memmove
# (CBMC built-in, which copies and bounds-checks correctly). No realloc here,
# so we do NOT link any string.c stub.
goto-cc -o ${FUNCTION}.goto "$FILE" --function ${FUNCTION}
goto-instrument --unwind ${UNWIND} --unwinding-assertions ${FUNCTION}.goto ${FUNCTION}.goto
# Inline editorFreeRow's body (three free() calls) rather than replacing it with
# its contract: the contract's `ensures __CPROVER_was_freed(...)` has no body
# under --replace-call-with-contract.  The precondition makes each row buffer a
# fresh, offset-zero heap object, so the inlined free()s are well-defined and
# editorDelRow's frees clause is checked against the real deallocations.
goto-instrument --nondet-static \
  --enforce-contract ${FUNCTION} ${FUNCTION}.goto checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto
cbmc checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto --function ${FUNCTION} \
  --no-malloc-may-fail --bounds-check --pointer-check --pointer-primitive-check \
  --pointer-overflow-check --conversion-check --signed-overflow-check --unsigned-overflow-check

#!/bin/bash
# Parallel mutation kill-score harness for editorDelRow.
# Mirrors verify_editorDelRow.sh EXACTLY (same unwind, same checks, same
# enforce-contract pipeline) so a "kill" means the canonical verification of the
# spec fails on that mutant.  Each mutant runs in its own temp dir so the 17
# runs proceed concurrently (the box has plenty of cores/RAM).
# Usage: ./run_mutants_editorDelRow.sh [unwind]
UNWIND=${1:-128}
FUNCTION=editorDelRow
SRC=/app/kilo/kilo.c
ROOT=$(mktemp -d)

# Each entry: "id|lineno|sed-substitution".  Substitutions are anchored to the
# exact original token so they apply to exactly one site on that line.
declare -a MUTANTS=(
  "01_guard_lt|867|s@at >= E\.numrows@at < E.numrows@"
  "02_guard_le|867|s@at >= E\.numrows@at <= E.numrows@"
  "03_guard_gt|867|s@at >= E\.numrows@at > E.numrows@"
  "04_guard_eq|867|s@at >= E\.numrows@at == E.numrows@"
  "05_guard_ne|867|s@at >= E\.numrows@at != E.numrows@"
  "06_row_minus|868|s@= E\.row+at;@= E.row-at;@"
  "07_mm_dest_minus|870|s@memmove(E\.row+at,@memmove(E.row-at,@"
  "08_mm_src_atm1|870|s@,E\.row+at+1,@,E.row+at-1,@"
  "09_mm_src_minus|870|s@,E\.row+at+1,@,E.row-at+1,@"
  "10_mm_size_atp1|870|s@(E\.numrows-at-1)@(E.numrows-at+1)@"
  "11_mm_size_plusat|870|s@(E\.numrows-at-1)@(E.numrows+at-1)@"
  "12_loop_le|871|s@j < E\.numrows-1@j <= E.numrows-1@"
  "13_loop_gt|871|s@j < E\.numrows-1@j > E.numrows-1@"
  "14_loop_ge|871|s@j < E\.numrows-1@j >= E.numrows-1@"
  "15_loop_eq|871|s@j < E\.numrows-1@j == E.numrows-1@"
  "16_loop_ne|871|s@j < E\.numrows-1@j != E.numrows-1@"
  "17_loop_plus1|871|s@j < E\.numrows-1@j < E.numrows+1@"
)

run_one() {
  local id="$1" line="$2" sub="$3"
  local d="$ROOT/$id"
  mkdir -p "$d"
  cp "$SRC" "$d/kilo.c"
  sed -i "${line}${sub}" "$d/kilo.c"
  if diff -q "$SRC" "$d/kilo.c" >/dev/null; then
    echo "$id: SED-NOOP"; return
  fi
  cd "$d" || return
  if ! goto-cc -o m.goto kilo.c --function ${FUNCTION} > log 2>&1; then
    echo "$id: GOTOCC-FAIL"; return
  fi
  goto-instrument --unwind ${UNWIND} --unwinding-assertions m.goto m.goto >> log 2>&1
  goto-instrument --nondet-static --enforce-contract ${FUNCTION} m.goto c.goto >> log 2>&1
  cbmc c.goto --function ${FUNCTION} \
    --no-malloc-may-fail --bounds-check --pointer-check --pointer-primitive-check \
    --pointer-overflow-check --conversion-check --signed-overflow-check \
    --unsigned-overflow-check > cbmc.out 2>&1
  if grep -q "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL" cbmc.out; then
    echo "$id: SURVIVED"
  elif grep -q "VERIFICATION FAILED" cbmc.out; then
    echo "$id: KILLED"
  else
    echo "$id: ERROR ($(tail -1 cbmc.out))"
  fi
}

echo "ROOT=$ROOT"
pids=()
for m in "${MUTANTS[@]}"; do
  IFS='|' read -r id line sub <<< "$m"
  run_one "$id" "$line" "$sub" >> "$ROOT/results.txt" 2>&1 &
  pids+=($!)
done
wait
echo "=== RESULTS ==="
sort "$ROOT/results.txt"
killed=$(grep -c KILLED "$ROOT/results.txt")
surv=$(grep -c SURVIVED "$ROOT/results.txt")
echo "=== KILLED $killed / $((killed+surv)) (survived $surv) ==="

#!/bin/bash
# Test mutation kill score for editorRowHasOpenComment.
# Usage: ./run_mutants.sh <depth>
DEPTH=${1:-300}
FUNCTION=editorRowHasOpenComment
FILE=kilo.c
cp "$FILE" "${FILE}.orig"

# Each mutant is described by line number and sed substitution applied to that line.
declare -a MUTANTS=(
  "388|s@row->render\[row->rsize-1\] != '/'@row->render[row->rsize-1] == '/'@"
  "387|s@row->render\[row->rsize-2\] != '\*'@row->render[row->rsize-2] == '*'@"
  "387|s@row->rsize < 2@row->rsize <= 2@"
  "387|s@row->rsize < 2@row->rsize > 2@"
  "387|s@row->rsize < 2@row->rsize >= 2@"
  "387|s@row->rsize < 2@row->rsize == 2@"
  "387|s@row->rsize < 2@row->rsize != 2@"
  "386|s@row->hl\[row->rsize-1\] == HL_MLCOMMENT@row->hl[row->rsize-1] != HL_MLCOMMENT@"
  "388|s@row->render\[row->rsize-1\] != '/'@row->render[row->rsize+1] != '/'@"
  "387|s@row->render\[row->rsize-2\] != '\*'@row->render[row->rsize+2] != '*'@"
  "386|s@row->hl\[row->rsize-1\] == HL_MLCOMMENT@row->hl[row->rsize+1] == HL_MLCOMMENT@"
  "386|s@HL_MLCOMMENT &&@HL_MLCOMMENT ||@"
  "387|s@row->rsize < 2 ||@row->rsize < 2 \&\&@"
  "387|s@!= '\*' ||@!= '*' \&\&@"
  "386|s@row->rsize \&\&@row->rsize ||@"
  "386|s@row->hl \&\&@row->hl ||@"
)

killed=0
total=0
i=0
for m in "${MUTANTS[@]}"; do
  i=$((i+1))
  line="${m%%|*}"
  sub="${m#*|}"
  cp "${FILE}.orig" "$FILE"
  sed -i "${line}${sub}" "$FILE"
  if diff -q "${FILE}.orig" "$FILE" >/dev/null; then
    echo "MUTANT $i: SED-NOOP (substitution did not apply)"
    continue
  fi
  total=$((total+1))
  rm -f ${FUNCTION}.goto checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto
  out=$(goto-cc -o ${FUNCTION}.goto "$FILE" --function ${FUNCTION} 2>&1 \
    && goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 ${FUNCTION}.goto ${FUNCTION}.goto 2>&1 \
    && goto-instrument --enforce-contract ${FUNCTION} ${FUNCTION}.goto checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto 2>&1 \
    && cbmc checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto --function ${FUNCTION} --depth ${DEPTH} 2>&1)
  if echo "$out" | grep -q "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL"; then
    echo "MUTANT $i (line $line): SURVIVED"
  else
    echo "MUTANT $i (line $line): KILLED"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
cp "${FILE}.orig" "$FILE"
rm -f "${FILE}.orig"
echo "=== KILLED $killed / $total ==="

4	set -u
5	SRC=${1:-/app/kilo/kilo.c}

11	goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 $FN.goto $FN.goto > gi1.out 2>&1
12	goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract editorInsertRow --enforce-contract $FN $FN.goto checking-$FN-contracts.goto > gi2.out 2>&1 || { echo "INSTR_FAIL"; cat gi2.out; exit 2; }
13	cbmc checking-$FN-contracts.goto --function $FN --depth 200 > cbmc.out 2>&1
14	RC=$?
15	echo "CBMC_RC=$RC"
16	grep -E "no body for|VERIFICATION|FAILURE" cbmc.out | head -40
17	echo "WORKDIR=$W"

#!/bin/bash
# Replicate the EXACT canonical run_cbmc pipeline for editorOpen.
# Usage: ./_canon_run.sh <SRC.c> [workdir]
set -u
SRC=${1:-/app/kilo/kilo.c}
W=${2:-$(mktemp -d)}
FN=editorOpen
cd "$W" || exit 99
# Attempt 1: no macro prevention, no stubs.
goto-cc -o $FN.goto "$SRC" --function $FN > gc.out 2>&1 || { echo "GOTO_CC_FAIL"; cat gc.out; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 $FN.goto $FN.goto > gi1.out 2>&1
goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract editorInsertRow --enforce-contract $FN $FN.goto checking-$FN-contracts.goto > gi2.out 2>&1 || { echo "INSTR_FAIL"; cat gi2.out; exit 2; }
cbmc checking-$FN-contracts.goto --function $FN --depth 200 > cbmc.out 2>&1
RC=$?
echo "CBMC_RC=$RC"
grep -E "no body for|VERIFICATION|FAILURE" cbmc.out | head -40
echo "WORKDIR=$W"

- **Preconditions** — `row` is `is_fresh`, and each of `row->render`, `row->chars`, `row->hl` is `is_fresh` (offset-zero valid heap object, size 1 is sufficient since `free` only needs a valid base pointer).
- **`__CPROVER_assigns`** — `object_whole` of each of the three buffers. This was the key fix: CBMC models `free()` as writing the whole object, so freed targets must appear in the assigns set, not just the frees set (same pattern as `editorInsertRow` and `editorOpen` in this file). - **`__CPROVER_frees`** — exactly `row->render, row->chars, row->hl`.
- **Postconditions** — `__CPROVER_was_freed(__CPROVER_old(...))` for each buffer, asserting each is actually released (and bounding the function to free exactly these three).

I only inserted contract clauses; the C body is unchanged."}, "cbmc": {"verdict": "PASS", "is_function_verified": true, "returncode": 0, "timed_out": false, "failed_step": null}}

1	"""Run CBMC on a function.
2	
3	Usage:
4	    % run-cbmc --function <FUNCTION_NAME> \
5	                       --file <PATH_TO_C_FILE> \
6	                       [-I <PATH_TO_INCLUDE_DIR(S)>]...
7	"""

9	import argparse
10	import json
11	import shlex
12	import subprocess
13	import sys
14	from dataclasses import dataclass
15	from datetime import UTC, datetime
16	from enum import StrEnum
17	from pathlib import Path
18	from subprocess import TimeoutExpired

20	from tools.construct_call_graph import construct_call_graph
21	
22	from tools.util import (
23	    build_stub_index,
24	    get_in_file_callees_for,
25	    get_stub_paths_for,
26	    get_unstubbed_external_callees_for,
27	)
28	from tools.util.callgraph import CallGraph

30	# Char budget for failure responses. The harness persists full tool output to a file but
31	# previews only the head inline, so we keep responses bounded (a pathological CBMC run can
32	# emit hundreds of MB) and lead with the FAILURE lines (see `_format_failure_response`) so
33	# the verdict survives that head-only preview rather than being buried in the stdout tail.
34	_MAX_RESPONSE_CHARS = 100_000
35	
36	# Of the budget left after the header, the leading FAILURE lines, and section labels, this
37	# fraction is given to the stdout tail; the remainder goes to the stderr tail.
38	_STDOUT_TAIL_SHARE = 0.7
39	
40	_DISABLE_MACRO_FLAGS = [
41	    "-D__NO_CTYPE"  # for ctype.h
42	]
43	
44	# 10-minute timeout.
45	_DEFAULT_RUN_CBMC_TIMEOUT_SEC = 600
46	
47	# goto-cc is fast; if it hasn't finished in this many seconds, the input is almost
48	# certainly pathological and should be treated as uncompilable.
49	_GOTO_CC_TIMEOUT_SEC = 30
50	
51	# GNU `timeout(1)` convention: distinguishes a timeout from a CBMC verification failure
52	# both in the CLI exit code and in the JSONL invocation log.
53	_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE = 124
54	
55	# Fixed values for CBMC's `--depth` and `--unwind` flags.
56	_CBMC_UNWIND = 5
57	_CBMC_DEPTH = 200
58	
59	
60	class CbmcStep(StrEnum):
61	    """Logical step in the CBMC verification pipeline.
62	
63	    `run_cbmc` runs the pipeline as three logical steps; each maps to one or more
64	    underlying subprocess invocations. When a step fails, the result names the step
65	    so callers can distinguish e.g. a goto-cc compile rejection from a cbmc
66	    verification failure.
67	    """
68	
69	    GOTO_CC = "goto-cc"
70	    GOTO_INSTRUMENT = "goto-instrument"
71	    CBMC = "cbmc"
72	
73	
74	@dataclass(frozen=True)
75	class RunCbmcResult:
76	    """The outcome of running CBMC on a function.
77	
78	    Attributes:
79	        function (str): The function under verification.
80	        failed_step (CbmcStep | None): The pipeline step that failed, or None on success.
81	        timed_out (bool): True iff any step hit the per-attempt timeout.
82	        returncode (int): 0 on success, `_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE` on timeout, otherwise the
83	            exit code of the failing subprocess (cbmc's verification exit code when
84	            `failed_step` is CBMC).
85	        response (str): Printable summary — a success line or a truncated failure block.
86	    """
87	
88	    function: str
89	    failed_step: CbmcStep | None
90	    timed_out: bool
91	    returncode: int
92	    response: str
93	
94	    @property
95	    def cbmc_ran_successfully(self) -> bool:
96	        """True iff the full pipeline ran without any step failing or timing out.
97	
98	        Note: This is *not* equivalent to a successful verification result. See
99	        `is_function_verified`.
100	
101	        """
102	        return self.failed_step is None and not self.timed_out
103	
104	    @property
105	    def is_function_verified(self) -> bool:
106	        """True iff the function for this verification run is successfully verified.
107	
108	        A function is successfully verified if all components of the CBMC pipeline ran successfully
109	        and the return code is 0.
110	        """
111	        return self.cbmc_ran_successfully and self.returncode == 0
112	
113	    def __str__(self) -> str:
114	        """Return the string representation of this result, used for logging.
115	
116	        Returns:
117	            str: The string representation of this result, used for logging.
118	        """
119	        if failed_step := self.failed_step:
120	            return f"{failed_step.value.upper()}_FAILED"
121	        if self.timed_out:
122	            return "TIMED_OUT"
123	        return "PASS" if self.is_function_verified else "FAIL"
124	
125	
126	@dataclass(frozen=True)
127	class _StepRun:
128	    """Outcome of running one subprocess step.
129	
130	    Attributes:
131	        step (CbmcStep): The logical step in the CBMC verification pipeline this subprocess belongs
132	            to.
133	        command (str): The shell command that was run.
134	        returncode (int): The subprocess exit code, or `_TIMEOUT_RETURNCODE` on timeout.
135	        stdout (str): Captured stdout (empty on timeout).
136	        stderr (str): Captured stderr (empty on timeout).
137	        timed_out (bool): True iff the subprocess hit the per-step timeout.
138	    """
139	
140	    step: CbmcStep
141	    command: str
142	    returncode: int
143	    stdout: str
144	    stderr: str
145	    timed_out: bool
146	
147	    @property
148	    def succeeded(self) -> bool:
149	        """True iff the subprocess exited zero and did not time out."""
150	        return self.returncode == 0 and not self.timed_out
151
